C2_verdicts.repro_1.6969_vs_physical_1.6875: 0.557% (paper Table 2 prints this pairing as 0.30%: arithmetically inconsistent; 0.30% only holds against the model-internal 1.6969)

C3_Q_predicted_identity.verdict: Q_predicted = m_e/(H/Q): its 0.56% gap vs e_nat is the H/Q gap vs the physical target restated, not an independent prediction

C4_R_phys.note: 191 fm requires H_code = 0.4946 (equivalently Q_code = 0.2928 at H/Q = 1.6890), neither printed in v11; hbar_c/191 fm = 1.0331 MeV coincides numerically with the m_J = 1.033 MeV of the July 5/8 papers

C5_ansatz_degenerate.verdict: the Section 5.1 production ansatz as printed vanishes identically for radial phi(r); the actual field configuration exists only in the benchmark code

el_of_printed_lagrangian_in_lorenz_gauge.mostly_plus: box A = J/4, box J = A/4 - g(J.J)J

C6_EL_normalization.verdict: the printed EL pair (2.1)/(2.2) is NOT the EL system of the printed Lagrangian -F^2 - G^2 + J.A - g(J.J)^2 under either signature; the unique reconstruction preserving the printed interaction signs is L_ref = -1/4 F^2 - 1/4 G^2 + J.A - g(J.J)^2 under mostly-plus signature (-,+,+,+); under (+,-,-,-) the coupling and quartic signs must flip as well; v11 states no metric signature; the dropped 1/4 cannot be absorbed by rescaling L because the quartic coefficient -4g in (2.2) pins the scale
